behavior_summary: Implements an 'earth' spell family that can be triggered by area/use events. On activation the handler checks the caster's mana and either runs a low-mana visual-only sequence or consumes mana to produce gameplay effects (award intelligence, process the spell target, and possibly create/animate objects or NPCs).

When mana is sufficient the main flow subtracts the spell cost (1 or 2 depending on variant), grants intelligence XP, resolves the spell target (existing item/NPC or none), and applies special handling: calls an item's use if it's a special type (type 0x026F) under a temporary deathSpeakCast flag, or makes a dead NPC 'bark' a death noise.

If no explicit target is returned by the target resolution routine the complex placement routine creates/positions a new item (corpse/object), may populate it (Item::pop), can spawn and initialize an NPC (Npc::create + stat/hp setup), or falls back/cleans up if placement fails. Helper variants temporarily make the caster immortal or feign death and sequence additional timed spawns (including scheduling a firespell).
notable_control_flow: Entry guard: handler runs only when the script's activation/info byte equals 1 (fast-area/activation event).
Mana branch: read Npc::getMana(); if below threshold run visual-only path (spawn directional effects, return); else subtract cost via Npc::setMana and continue to effect path.
Sequenced effects: visual/motion spawns (METHOD::15E7 and FREE::29D8) are performed with suspends/delays (FREE::1A96/FREE::1C19) to pace the sequence.

Target processing branch: call 'process target()' -> if result==0 return; if item.type==0x026F set global deathSpeakCast=1 then call Item::use then clear flag; else if Item::isNpc && Npc::isDead then Item::bark("Arrrgghhh!").
Placement fallbacks: complex placement loop uses canExistAt to find valid coordinates and will Item::destroy created objects on failure.
open_questions: Exact numeric type meaning for item type 0x026F (inference: triggers a 'death speak' use) — behavior is observed but concrete type name is unknown.
Precise parameters and timing values for external FREE::* tasks and motion spawns (their gameplay durations/effects are inferred from usage but not enumerated).
Whether some variants are reachable only under particular game-mode conditions (e.g., avatar stasis and global stasis flags interaction are inferred but not exhaustively proven).
Full set of side-effects other systems read from deathSpeakCast (we see it written but not read here).

main_function.name: U8P_ERTHSPEL::0080 (Func_80)
main_function.purpose: Handle an earth-spell activation: check caster mana, run a visual-only sequence if mana is low or consume mana to award intelligence, resolve/process the spell target, and react to or create/animate targets (special-case item types and dead NPCs).
entry_conditions: Triggered when the script's info byte/activation flag equals 1 (enterFastArea/activation event).
Requires a pid (process/player id) and an item_ptr parameter (triggering item) in context.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHSPEL::0080 (Func_80)
function_map.role: Primary earth-spell activation handler that implements mana checks, effect spawning, target processing, and special-case reactions.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHSPEL::01AF (Func_1AF)
function_map.role: Variant of the main handler which runs the same casting logic but sets the caster to feign-death at the end.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHSPEL::0263 (Func_263)
function_map.role: Short helper that sets the caster immortal for a duration, delays, spawns effects, then clears immortal (used to protect the caster during sequences).

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHSPEL::02BF (Func_2BF)
function_map.role: Alt casting variant that uses a mana threshold of 2, consumes 2 mana on success, awards intelligence, and spawns the immortal helper at the end.

function_map.name: U8P_ERTHSPEL::0379 (Func_379)
function_map.role: Complex placement/creation routine invoked by the spell to create/populate items or spawn NPCs, including robust placement checks, initialization, SFX, and follow-up firespell scheduling.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn/call the temporary-immortal helper after a successful 2-mana cast to protect the caster during subsequent sequences.
The main handler or its variants may invoke the complex placement/creation routine when target processing indicates no existing target; this is the placement/summon path.

external_calls.purpose: Read caster's current mana to decide low-mana visual-only path vs mana-consuming effect path.
Subtract spell cost from caster's mana when enough mana is available.
Get caster facing direction to orient directional visual/motion spawns.
Spawn directional motion/visual effects oriented by caster direction.
Spawn a timed/generic effect used alongside directional spawns (particle/timed visual).
Small startup/free routine executed at activation for setup or animation preparation.
Short delay/sleep used before awarding intelligence or pacing effects.
Grant intelligence experience/points to the caster when mana is consumed.
Resolve the spell's target (returning an item/NPC handle or 0) to decide follow-up actions.
Inspect target item type to special-case behavior (e.g., type 0x026F death-speak).
Invoke special item behavior under a temporary global context (deathSpeakCast).
Detect whether the processed target is an NPC to call NPC-specific checks/actions.
If the target NPC is dead, trigger a death bark (flavor reaction).
Emit short text from an item/NPC (used for the dead-NPC "Arrrgghhh!" line and other barks).
Put the caster into a feign-death state at the end of the variant's sequence.
Temporarily mark caster as immortal at the start of the helper sequence.
Delay/sleep while caster remains immortal.
Clear the temporary immortal state at the end of the helper.
Create a world item (e.g., corpse/object) to place if no explicit target exists.
Populate/place the created item into the world or container at chosen coordinates.
Verify map placement validity for created items/NPCs and loop to find a valid spot.
Cleanup created items if placement fails.
Spawn an NPC when the placement routine reanimates or summons a creature.
Notify/update engine state after creating/populating items.
Play sound effects tied to creation/impact events in the placement sequence.
Schedule or spawn a fire-spell effect as a follow-up in compound spell behavior.
Compute orientation for spawned NPCs/items relative to caster/target coordinates.
Query global avatar stasis before optionally setting/clearing stasis during the sequence.
Set the avatar into stasis to freeze movement during sensitive placement steps (inferred).

writes.name: deathSpeakCast (global [002A 01])
writes.likely_effect: Temporarily set to 1 to signal a 'death-speak' context immediately before calling Item::use on type 0x026F items, then cleared to 0 after the call. Used to influence item.use behavior across systems.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements an 'earth' spell family that can be triggered by area/use events. On activation the handler checks the caster's mana and either runs a low-mana visual-only sequence or consumes mana to produce gameplay effects (award intelligence, process the spell target, and possibly create/animate objects or NPCs).
